chunk_summary: This section focuses on energy density counseling and texture safety in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months, emphasizing guidelines to prevent choking and ensure adequate nutrition during this critical developmental stage.
learning_objectives: Understand the importance of energy density in complementary feeding for infants aged 6–12 months.
Learn safe food texture practices to minimize choking risks in infants.
Apply WHO and UNICEF guidelines for complementary feeding to ensure optimal growth and development.
Recognize cultural and dietary considerations in complementary feeding practices.
key_facts: Infants aged 6–12 months require complementary foods with high energy density to meet nutritional needs.
WHO recommends introducing complementary foods at 6 months while continuing breastfeeding.
Choking risks can be minimized by offering soft, mashed, or appropriately textured foods suitable for infants.
UNICEF emphasizes the importance of responsive feeding practices to encourage healthy eating habits.
ICMR-NIN guidelines suggest culturally appropriate complementary feeding practices tailored to Indian diets.
reference_quote: WHO recommends introducing complementary foods at 6 months of age while continuing breastfeeding to ensure adequate nutrition and development. (Source: https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK596430/)
chunk_heading: DECISIONS  IN  INFANTS  SIX  TO  TWELVE  MONTHS;ENERGY  DENSITY  COUNSELING AND  TEXTURE SAFETY IN COMPLEMENTARY FEEDING;
